page: Microsoft Azure B1ms is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single vCPU mapped to one physical core and one thread, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with x86_64 architecture running at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512 instructions. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and 4 GB of local storage, and it supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates that the B1ms is positioned at the lower end of the performance spectrum. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance scored 1224.89, placing it in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 73.5 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 9.24 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 7.48 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Passmark CPU and memory scores also fall into the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

The primary tradeoff of the B1ms is its burstable CPU allocation model, which is designed for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. While this model offers qualitative cost efficiency for low-demand or intermittent tasks, the hardware exhibits poor throughput under sustained loads. For example, static web serving throughput is limited to 1.47 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reach 1.5 million operations per second, both falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarked systems.

Given these hardware constraints and benchmark results, the B1ms is suitable for low-traffic web servers, small databases with minimal transaction volumes, development and testing environments, and basic microservices that experience periodic idle times. It is not suited for high-throughput database operations, intensive data processing, or sustained compute workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure B1ms is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, powered by a single-core Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2.0 GB of RAM, 4 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Designed for budget-conscious and low-utilization scenarios, its burstable CPU allocation model trades sustained performance for cost efficiency. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower tier of performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores at 1224.89, and uncached memory read bandwidth at 9.24 GB/sec. Consequently, the B1ms is best suited for light workloads such as small development environments, low-traffic web servers, and minor microservices that require intermittent CPU bursts rather than continuous high-throughput processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B1ms is a burstable virtual machine featuring 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 4 GB of local storage, designed for low-utilization workloads and budget-conscious environments.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B1ms is a burstable virtual machine with 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 4 GB storage, designed for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable single-core virtual machine designed for low-utilization workloads, development environments, and light microservices.
bullet_points: Single-core Intel Xeon 8171M processor at 2.6 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 4 GB of local storage
Burstable CPU allocation model for intermittent workloads
Premium Storage capable under Microsoft Hyper-V
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, development, and testing
